rules.effect: Raw command keywords are not safety evidence until the context classifier marks actual_command.
Keywords inside plans, quoted logs, tool output, or hooks count as noise pressure, not direct safety regression.
Weak adoption proxies are held for review unless resolved or strong acceptance evidence exists.
Corrections, failure reports, user rejection, and takeover become rejection-ledger evidence before adoption scoring.
Annotated unsafe boundaries and actual risky commands remain boundary-review cases.
SWE-rebench parquet coverage remains a blocked coverage issue until readable per-sample records exist.

parameter_table.label: Safety-first
parameter_table.intent: Prefer boundary review unless context is clearly non-actual noise.

parameter_table.label: Balanced context
parameter_table.intent: Filter clear keyword noise while preserving actual-risk and unsafe boundaries.

parameter_table.label: Noise tolerant
parameter_table.intent: Filter non-actual command keywords aggressively, but keep actual commands blocked.

parameter_table.label: Noise tolerant + pushback strict
parameter_table.intent: Keep aggressive non-actual command noise filtering while mapping any user pushback before adoption scoring.

parameter_table.label: Adoption lenient
parameter_table.intent: Trust weak adopted proxies more quickly; useful as a stress test, not the default.

parameter_table.label: Risk-keyword lenient
parameter_table.intent: Require repeated actual-risk keywords before boundary review; expected to expose unsafe relaxations.

candidates.label: Noise tolerant + pushback strict
candidates.intent: Keep aggressive non-actual command noise filtering while mapping any user pushback before adoption scoring.

candidates.label: Noise tolerant
candidates.intent: Filter non-actual command keywords aggressively, but keep actual commands blocked.

candidates.intent: Filter clear keyword noise while preserving actual-risk and unsafe boundaries.

diagnostic_notes: Actual-risk command threshold has independent samples without unsafe labels.
No blocked parquet coverage issue was present in this batch.

policy_channels.readout_effect: annotate non-execution command-pattern pressure in shadow reports
annotate complex traces that need deeper review or evidence budget
allowed_downstream_uses: prioritize complex traces for future offline inspection
blocked_downstream_uses: treat tool volume as success evidence
treat tool volume as safety evidence

policy_channels.readout_effect: annotate failed-outcome damping pressure without relying on unsafe labels
allowed_downstream_uses: raise shadow damping pressure when failure evidence is present
prioritize offline inspection of failed outcomes that are not unsafe-label shortcuts
blocked_downstream_uses: treat failed outcome as automatic production rejection

policy_channels.readout_effect: annotate evidence-budget pressure when command-looking noise overlaps failed outcome evidence
allowed_downstream_uses: prioritize sanitized holdout inspection for noisy failed traces
blocked_downstream_uses: convert command noise plus failure into automatic rejection

policy_channels.readout_effect: annotate rejection-damping pressure when user pushback overlaps weak or failed proxy evidence
allowed_downstream_uses: explain pushback and weak-or-failed proxy conflict
prioritize future offline review of pushback-heavy traces
blocked_downstream_uses: let pushback alone become winner authority

closure_checks.name: source shadow policy surface is ready
selected profile matches source policy surface
only shadow readout channels are consumed
blocked alpha stays out of consumed channels
side-by-side consumption changes no actions or authority
side-by-side consumption has no persistence or provider effects

warnings: This side-by-side report is a Layer 3 calibration draft; it does not replace production rules.
Scores are proxy diagnostics over sanitized Layer 2 records, not final model-quality proof.
Zilliz and embeddings stay off until sanitized evidence payloads are separately reviewed.
SWE-rebench remains a coverage gap until parquet is converted or a local reader is added.

checks.name: no live writes or provider calls
calibrated rules preserve unsafe and actual command boundaries
